comments.body: Create a class that implements Predicate, and create an instance of the class.
Are you asking how to implement an interface in Java?
Possible duplicate of How to implement this FilteringIterator?

Would have been helpful to see client code which iterated through your custom list since I don’t understand how filtered iterator is passed via constructor; usually we do myList.iterator() to get implementation of iterator to iterate through elements
Does this answer your question? How to implement this FilteringIterator?
@dnault it would be nice to implement - but it wasn't part of the question... Second, after seeing how next is implemented - it shouldn't be too difficult to do similar thing for hasNext.
This answer helped, I didnt create an object implementing Predicate.

answers.body:
Sounds like you want to implement next() in FilteringIterator class. It should be something like:

public T next() {
    T next = iterf.next();
    while (!pred.accept(next))
        next = iterf.next();
    return next;
}

Now that you see next() it should be an easy exercise to implement hasNext() as well!

In order to create a predicate you should implement Predicate interface, for example:

class MyPredicate implements Predicate {

    @Override
    public boolean accept(Object ob){
        if (!ob instanceof Student)
            return false;

        Student student = (Student) ob;
        return student.a > 1;
    }

}

Here's a nice way to make it generic with any iterator:

public class FilteringIterator<T> implements Iterator<T> {
  // to distinguish between "null" from source iterator and "no object in buffer"
  private static final T SENTINEL_EMPTY = (T) new Object();

  private final Iterator<? extends T> source;
  private final Predicate<? super T> predicate;

  // will only ever hold a valid value (including null, if the predicate permits), or SENTINEL_EMPTY
  private T buffer = SENTINEL_EMPTY;

  public FilteringIterator(Iterator<? extends T> source, Predicate<? super T> predicate) {
    this.source = source;
    this.predicate = predicate;
  }

  @Override
  public boolean hasNext() {
    if (buffer != SENTINEL_EMPTY) return true;
    while (buffer == SENTINEL_EMPTY && source.hasNext()) {
      T tempNext = source.next();
      if (predicate.test(tempNext)) {
        buffer = tempNext;
      }
    }
    // If we ran through all remaining elements in the source iterator 
    // without finding a match, the buffer will still be the sentinel
    return buffer != SENTINEL_EMPTY;
  }

  @Override
  public T next() {
    if (buffer != SENTINEL_EMPTY) {
      T temp = buffer;
      buffer = SENTINEL_EMPTY;
      return temp;
    }
    while (source.hasNext()) {
      T nextElement = source.next();
      if (predicate.test(nextElement)) {
        return nextElement;
      }
    }
    throw new NoSuchElementException("No more elements exist in source iterator " + source + " that match predicate " + predicate);
  }
}

You can use it like this:

List<Integer> list = List.of(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6);
Iterator<Integer> filteredIterator = new FilteringIterator(list.iterator(), i -> i % 2 == 0);
while (filteredIterator.hasNext()) {
  System.out.println(filteredIterator.next());
}

which prints

2
4
6

title: How to implement Filtering Iterator in Java?
body:
I have created an array (tab[]) of objects and now I need to filter and print via one of their fields (ex. print if ob.a>1 ). I created an Iterator interface, ArrayIterator that goes through all entries on list and Predicate interface. This is constructor of my FilteringIterator:

private final Iterator iterf;
private final Predicate pred; 
public FilteringIterator(ArrayIterator i, Predicate predykat) 
{ 
    iterf=i;
    pred=predykat;
    iterf.first();
}

Method that prints every entry using my "basic" iterator looks like this:

  public void printlist()
{
    ArrayIterator itab =new ArrayIterator(lista);
    for(itab.first();!itab.isDone(); itab.next())
    {
        Student stud=(Student)itab.current();
        stud.show();
    }
}

I also have a class that implements Predicate interface:

public interface Predicate

 {
      public boolean accept(Object ob); 
 }

How to use my FilteringIterator, when it requires Predicate, and I cannot create such object, as it's an interface?
